source.preservationPolicy: Raw page text is included so no original PDF content is lost. Structured topics are cleaned for app use.

rawPages.text:
Dams and Rivers GK
Making notes for GA, Considering static GK Geography for
Indian competitive exams
What are most imp, most asked, favorite, strategic , Always
in news , important info.
Topic - Dams and Rivers
I’ll make this exam-notes style: high-yield dam–river–state mapping first, then “why asked,”
news-linked facts, and quick memory hooks for revision.
Static GK Geography: Dams & Rivers — Most Important for Indian
Exams
For SSC, Railways, DSSSB, State PSC, UPSC prelims-type GK, dams are usually asked in this
format:
Dam → River → State
Project → River/State
Largest/highest/oldest/famous dam
Hydroelectric / multipurpose project
News-linked river projects
CWC maintains India’s official National Register of Large Dams, so dam facts are a
standard static GK area.
Central Water C… +1
1. Top 30 Most Asked Dams: Must Memorise
Dam / Project River State Why Important
Bhakra Nangal Dam Sutlej Himachal Pradesh / Very frequent;
Punjab multipurpose project
Hirakud Dam Mahanadi Odisha One of the longest
earthen dams
Sardar Sarovar Dam Narmada Gujarat Narmada Valley Project;
always in news
Tehri Dam Bhagirathi Uttarakhand Highest dam in India
1/15

Dam / Project River State Why Important

Dam Pradesh project

Stanley Reservoir relevance

Koyna Dam Koyna Maharashtra Major hydroelectric
project

Memorise these first:

3. “Favorite” Exam Questions
These are repeatedly asked:
Highest dam in India
Tehri Dam — on Bhagirathi River, Uttarakhand.
Longest major dam often asked
Hirakud Dam — on Mahanadi River, Odisha.
Largest reservoir by installed/known GK association
Indira Sagar Dam — on Narmada River, Madhya Pradesh.
Famous arch dam
Idukki Dam — on Periyar River, Kerala.
4/15

Dam linked to Narmada Bachao Andolan
Sardar Sarovar Dam — on Narmada River, Gujarat.
Damodar Valley Corporation dams
Remember: Maithon, Panchet, Tilaiya, Konar.
DVC is called India’s early multipurpose river valley project model.
Chambal Valley Project dams
Gandhi Sagar → Rana Pratap Sagar → Jawahar Sagar → Kota Barrage
Kaveri dispute dam
Mettur Dam — Tamil Nadu, on Kaveri.
Periyar dispute dam
Mullaperiyar Dam — on Periyar, located in Kerala but operated by Tamil Nadu; often in
news due to safety/water-sharing dispute.
4. Dams “Always in News” — Strategic List
These are important because they connect static GK + current affairs:
Dam/Project River Why in News/Strategic
Subansiri Lower Subansiri Major 2,000 MW hydropower project; India’s
largest hydropower project as per PIB release on
commercial operation milestone.
Press Informatio…

Kwar Chenab News-linked J&K hydro project
Sawalkote Chenab Large J&K hydropower project recently
cleared/revived in news. The Economic Ti…
Kishanganga Kishanganga/Neelu India–Pakistan water dispute

displacement/environment
Farakka Barrage Ganga India–Bangladesh water sharing, sedimentation
Yarlung Tsangpo Brahmaputra China’s mega dam concerns for India/Bangladesh.
mega dam upstream in Tibet The Guardian

Chambal chain: Gandhi → Rana → Jawahar → Kota
6. One-Liner Facts for Exams
1. Tehri Dam is on the Bhagirathi River in Uttarakhand.
2. Bhakra Nangal Dam is on the Sutlej River.
3. Hirakud Dam is on the Mahanadi River in Odisha.
4. Sardar Sarovar Dam is on the Narmada River in Gujarat.
5. Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is on the Krishna River.
6. Mettur Dam is on the Kaveri River in Tamil Nadu.
7. Idukki Dam is on the Periyar River in Kerala.
8/15

8. Koyna Dam is on the Koyna River in Maharashtra.
9. Ukai Dam is on the Tapi/Tapti River in Gujarat.
10. Farakka Barrage is on the Ganga River in West Bengal.
11. Mullaperiyar Dam is on the Periyar River and is linked with Kerala–Tamil Nadu
dispute.
12. Baglihar, Salal, Ratle, Dulhasti are linked with the Chenab River.
13. Kishanganga Project is linked with Kishanganga/Neelum River.
14. Polavaram Project is on the Godavari River.
15. Subansiri Lower Project is on the Subansiri River in the Northeast.
7. Most Important Match-the-Following Set
Practice this:
Column A Column B
Tehri Bhagirathi
Bhakra Nangal Sutlej
Hirakud Mahanadi
Sardar Sarovar Narmada
Nagarjuna Sagar Krishna
Mettur Kaveri
Idukki Periyar
Koyna Maharashtra
Gandhi Sagar Chambal
Maithon Barakar
Polavaram Godavari
Farakka Ganga
Baglihar Chenab
Kishanganga Neelum/Kishanganga
9/15

Column A Column B
Ukai Tapi
8. Best Revision Strategy
For competitive exams, don’t try to memorise 100+ dams randomly. Use this order:
Priority 1: Top 30 dams table
Priority 2: River-wise groups: Narmada, Krishna, Kaveri, Chenab, Chambal, Damodar
Priority 3: Superlatives: highest, longest, famous arch dam, largest reservoir
Priority 4: News dams: Subansiri, Ratle, Pakal Dul, Sawalkote, Mullaperiyar, Polavaram,
Farakka
Priority 5: Map practice: locate river + state
For one-day revision, focus only on Dam → River → State and the news-linked dams.
SSoouurrcceess
topic: articles in consittution
1. RBI – banking, NBFCs, monetary policy, payments.
2. SEBI – stock market, mutual funds, brokers, listed companies.
3. IRDAI – insurance sector: life, general, health insurance.
4. PFRDA – pension sector; regulates NPS.
5. NABARD – agriculture/rural credit, cooperative banks, RRB support.
6. SIDBI – MSME finance and development.
7. NHB – housing finance sector support/regulation link.
8. TRAI – telecom tariffs, operators, consumer protection.
9. CCI – prevents anti-competitive practices, monopoly abuse.
10. FSSAI – food safety and food standards.
11. BIS – quality standards, ISI mark, hallmarking standards.
12. DGCA – civil aviation safety and aircraft operations.
13. CERC/SERC – electricity tariff and power regulation.
14. AERA – airport economic regulation.
15. PNGRB – petroleum and natural gas pipelines/city gas.
16. CPCB/SPCB – pollution control and environmental standards.
17. UGC – higher education universities.
18. AICTE – technical education institutions.
19. NMC – medical education and doctors’ registration.
20. Bar Council of India – legal education and advocate regulation.
10/15

topic: Organs vitamins & diseases
1. Vitamin A – eyes/vision; deficiency: night blindness.
2. Vitamin B1 – nerves; deficiency: beriberi.
3. Vitamin B2 – skin/mouth; deficiency: cheilosis, mouth cracks.
4. Vitamin B3 – skin/nerves; deficiency: pellagra.
5. Vitamin B9 – blood/RBC formation; deficiency: anaemia.
6. Vitamin B12 – nerves + RBC; deficiency: pernicious anaemia.
7. Vitamin C – gums/wound healing; deficiency: scurvy.
8. Vitamin D – bones/calcium absorption; deficiency: rickets/osteomalacia.
9. Vitamin E – reproductive health/antioxidant; deficiency: sterility rare.
10. Vitamin K – blood clotting; deficiency: excess bleeding.
11. Iodine – thyroid gland; deficiency: goitre.
12. Iron – haemoglobin; deficiency: anaemia.
13. Calcium – bones/teeth; deficiency: weak bones, osteoporosis.
14. Liver – produces bile; disease: jaundice, hepatitis.
15. Kidney – filters blood, forms urine; disease: nephritis, kidney stone.
16. Heart – pumps blood; disease: heart attack, hypertension.
17. Lungs – respiration; disease: TB, pneumonia, asthma.
18. Pancreas – insulin secretion; disease: diabetes mellitus.
19. Brain – control centre; disease: stroke, meningitis, epilepsy.
20. Stomach/intestine – digestion/absorption; disease: ulcer, diarrhoea, cholera, typhoid.
Topic: imp phy + chem from ssc bank POV
Important National & International Days (Top 20 SSC/Bank)
1. 12 January – National Youth Day (Swami Vivekananda Birth Anniversary)
2. 24 January – National Girl Child Day
3. 26 January – Republic Day (India)
4. 28 February – National Science Day
5. 8 March – International Women's Day
6. 22 March – World Water Day
7. 7 April – World Health Day
8. 22 April – Earth Day
9. 1 May – International Labour Day / May Day
10. 21 May – National Anti-Terrorism Day
11/15

11. 5 June – World Environment Day
12. 21 June – International Day of Yoga
13. 11 July – World Population Day
14. 15 August – Independence Day (India)
15. 29 August – National Sports Day (Major Dhyan Chand Birth Anniversary)
16. 5 September – Teachers' Day (Sarvepalli Radhakrishnan Birth Anniversary)
17. 8 September – International Literacy Day
18. 2 October – Gandhi Jayanti & International Day of Non-Violence
19. 14 November – Children's Day (Jawaharlal Nehru Birth Anniversary)
20. 10 December – Human Rights Day
Extra Super-Important (Often Asked)
23 January – Parakram Diwas (Subhas Chandra Bose)
31 October – National Unity Day (Vallabhbhai Patel)
14 April – Ambedkar Jayanti
1 December – World AIDS Day
16 September – World Ozone Day
9 August – Quit India Movement Day / August Kranti Day
25 January – National Voters' Day
24 October – United Nations Day
11 October – International Day of the Girl Child
3 December – International Day of Persons with Disabilities
Ultra-Short Revision
Jan: Youth, Republic
Feb: Science
Mar: Women, Water
Apr: Health, Earth, Ambedkar
Jun: Environment, Yoga
Aug: Independence, Sports
Sep: Teachers, Literacy
Oct: Gandhi, UN
Nov: Children's Day
Dec: AIDS, Human Rights.
Topic: HQ, City < Reports Wrt CUrrent Affairs
For SSC + Banking, don't memorize 200 organizations. These 20 HQ + City + Report
combinations cover a huge portion of questions.
12/15

HQ + City + Famous Report (20 Points)
1. International Monetary Fund – Washington DC – World Economic Outlook (WEO).
2. World Bank – Washington DC – World Development Report.
3. World Economic Forum – Cologny (Geneva), Switzerland – Global Competitiveness
Report.
4. United Nations Development Programme – New York – Human Development
Report (HDI).
5. United Nations Environment Programme – Nairobi – Emissions Gap Report.
6. World Health Organization – Geneva – World Health Statistics.
7. Food and Agriculture Organization – Rome – State of Food Security Report.
8. United Nations Children's Fund – New York – State of the World's Children.
9. United Nations Conference on Trade and Development – Geneva – World
Investment Report.
10. International Labour Organization – Geneva – World Employment and Social
Outlook.
11. Transparency International – Berlin – Corruption Perceptions Index.
12. World Intellectual Property Organization – Geneva – Global Innovation Index.
13. Reporters Without Borders – Paris – World Press Freedom Index.
14. Institute for Economics and Peace – Sydney – Global Peace Index.
15. International Energy Agency – Paris – World Energy Outlook.
16. Reserve Bank of India – Mumbai – Financial Stability Report.
17. NITI Aayog – New Delhi – SDG India Index.
18. National Statistical Office – New Delhi – GDP Estimates & Survey Reports.
19. Securities and Exchange Board of India – Mumbai – SEBI Annual Report.
20. State Bank of India – Mumbai – Ecowrap Report.
One-Line Memory Hack
Washington → IMF + World Bank
Geneva → WHO + ILO + WIPO + UNCTAD
New York → UNDP + UNICEF
Paris → IEA + Press Freedom Index
Mumbai → RBI + SEBI + SBI
New Delhi → NITI Aayog + NSO
These 20 are the ones most likely to appear in Banking GA, SSC CGL, CHSL, CPO, IBPS PO,
Clerk, RRB, SBI PO, and Insurance exams.
Recent National, intenrational,Technical,Sports news
(everything sbout them
13/15

, ) : dat alimti 50 wrt to Bank ssc
I’ll make this as a 50-point current-affairs notes sheet, focused on SSC/Bank exam-style
facts: national, international, tech/space, economy and sports.
As of 21 June 2026, Bank/SSC exam-focused 50 current affairs points:
1. India to host 11th BRICS Energy Ministers’ Meeting – Gurugram, 25–26 June 2026.
2. BRICS NSA Meeting under India’s chairship – 22–23 June 2026.
3. International Day of Yoga 2026 – celebrated on 21 June; important national +
international event.
4. PM attended G7 Summit 2026 in France; India was invited as partner country.
Ministry of Exter…
5. India–Slovakia Joint Statement – released during PM visit in June 2026.
6. India–Africa Forum Summit IV – hosted in New Delhi in 2026.
Ministry of Exter…
7. India–Myanmar Joint Statement 2026 – focused on trade, economy, defence and
security.
Press Informatio…
8. India’s space economy projected around USD 45 billion in next decade.
Press Informatio…
9. India has 400+ space startups – important for startup/current affairs.
Press Informatio…
10. India’s startup ecosystem generated nearly 25 lakh jobs.
Press Informatio…
11. India’s bioeconomy rose from about $10 billion to $190 billion in 12 years.
Press Informatio…
12. Seven CSIR technologies licensed to industry – science-tech current affairs.
Press Informatio…
13. Quantum sensing components delivered to DRDO – defence-tech point.
Press Informatio…
14. ISRO START 2026 theme: Observation from Space.
ISRO
15. ISRO–IIG Space Weather Summer School 2026 held at IIG, Mumbai.
ISRO
16. ISRO PSLV-C62 / EOS-N1 Mission launched on 12 Jan 2026.
ISRO
17. NISAR mission was launched by GSLV-F16 on 30 July 2025; still important for 2026
exams.
ISRO
18. Gaganyaan remains India’s key human spaceflight mission.
19. Indian Microgravity Experiments IMEx-2026 linked to Gaganyaan/human spaceflight
research.
ISRO
20. SPADEX remains important for docking technology and future space station plans.
21. Public Sector Banks FY 2025–26 showed strong profitability and improved asset
quality.
Guidely
22. RBI Financial Stability Report – remember: released by RBI, HQ Mumbai.
23. GST Council / GST updates – always important for banking GA.
24. UPI, CBDC, digital payments – RBI-regulated payment ecosystem.
14/15

25. Financial inclusion + Jan Dhan + DBT remain high-yield banking themes.
26. India’s forex reserves, inflation, repo rate – revise monthly before exam.
27. NABARD – rural/agriculture credit; important for Bank + RRB.
28. SIDBI – MSME finance; important for banking awareness.
29. SEBI – capital market regulator; HQ Mumbai.
30. IRDAI – insurance regulator; HQ Hyderabad.
31. India won FIH Women’s Nations Cup 2026 by defeating New Zealand 2–0.
The Times of India
32. This win gave India promotion to FIH Pro League.
The Times of India
33. India completed unbeaten campaign in FIH Women’s Nations Cup 2026.
34. Shubman Gill won first ODI series as India captain against Afghanistan.
The Times of India
35. India defeated Afghanistan 3–0 in ODI series.
The Times of India
36. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scored 94 off 29 balls in India A tri-series final.
The Times of India
37. India A won tri-series title by defeating Sri Lanka A.
The Times of India
38. World Yogasana Championship 2026 held in Ahmedabad.
39. India topped World Yogasana Championship with 114 medals.
GKToday
40. Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 2026 began in Mysuru.
The Times of India
41. Artemis Programme – NASA Moon mission; revise Artemis II/III.
42. China’s Yarlung Tsangpo mega dam – important for India-Brahmaputra geopolitics.
43. Red Sea shipping crisis – linked to Bab-el-Mandeb, Suez route, global trade.
44. South China Sea dispute – China, Philippines, ASEAN; major trade route.
45. Russia–Ukraine war – energy, food security, NATO relevance.
46. Israel–Gaza / West Asia tensions – oil, Red Sea, global diplomacy.
47. BRICS expansion + India chairship events – important international grouping.
48. G7 2026 – India participated as partner country.
49. AI, semiconductors, quantum tech – top technology current affairs themes.
50. Green energy + climate finance + EVs + solar mission – top environment/economy
theme for SSC/Bank.

knownPdfIssues: Original PDF mixed Dams and Rivers notes with unrelated GK topics after page 10.
Original PDF included chat prompt text, assistant-style phrasing, broken citation chips and duplicated source labels.
Current-affairs section is preserved as source content but not fact-checked in this JSON.

topics.title: Dams and Rivers GK

sections.title: How Exam Questions Are Asked
items: Largest, highest, oldest or famous dam
News-linked river projects and interstate disputes

sections.title: Exam-Memory Notes
items: Damodar Valley Corporation dams: Maithon, Panchet, Tilaiya and Konar.
Chambal Valley sequence: Gandhi Sagar -> Rana Pratap Sagar -> Jawahar Sagar -> Kota Barrage.
Narmada set: Sardar Sarovar, Indira Sagar, Omkareshwar.
Kaveri set: KRS, Mettur, Kabini, Harangi, Kallanai.

topDams.whyImportant: Very frequent; multipurpose irrigation and power project.
Longest major earthen dam association; early multipurpose project.
Narmada Valley Project; linked with Narmada Bachao Andolan.
Important Krishna basin hydropower and irrigation project.
Kaveri basin; often asked with Mettur.
Located in Kerala; operated/maintained by Tamil Nadu; dispute in news.
Major hydroelectric project in western India.

topDams.damOrProject: Rihand Dam / Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar

topDams.whyImportant: First major dam in Chambal chain.
Barrage in the Chambal chain; not a dam.
National project; displacement and environment relevance.
Oldest functional water-diversion structure in India.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Sardar Sarovar - Gujarat; Indira Sagar, Omkareshwar - Madhya Pradesh.
riverWiseGroups.memoryHook: Memory: Narmada = Gujarat + MP = Sardar + Indira + Omkareshwar.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Nagarjuna Sagar - Telangana/AP; Srisailam - AP/Telangana; Almatti - Karnataka; Tungabhadra - Karnataka.
riverWiseGroups.memoryHook: Memory: Krishna basin = Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Karnataka.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Mettur - Tamil Nadu; Krishna Raja Sagar, Kabini, Harangi - Karnataka; Kallanai - Tamil Nadu.
riverWiseGroups.memoryHook: Memory: Kaveri dispute = Karnataka vs Tamil Nadu = KRS + Mettur.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Salal, Baglihar, Dulhasti, Ratle, Pakal Dul, Kiru, Kwar, Sawalkote - Jammu and Kashmir.
riverWiseGroups.memoryHook: Memory: Chenab = hydropower + Indus Waters Treaty questions.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Panchet - Damodar; Maithon and Tilaiya - Barakar; Konar - Konar.
riverWiseGroups.memoryHook: Memory: DVC = Maithon, Panchet, Tilaiya, Konar.

riverWiseGroups.damsOrProjects: Gandhi Sagar - Madhya Pradesh; Rana Pratap Sagar, Jawahar Sagar and Kota Barrage - Rajasthan.

newsLinkedStrategicProjects.whyItMatters: Large 2,000 MW hydropower project; Northeast infrastructure and environment questions.
Run-of-river hydropower project; Indus basin/current-affairs relevance.

Jammu and Kashmir hydro projects; learn with Ratle and Pakal Dul.
Large proposed/revived Chenab hydro project; appears in news.
Safety and water-sharing dispute between Kerala and Tamil Nadu.
National project; displacement, rehabilitation and environment relevance.
India-Bangladesh water sharing, siltation and Hooghly navigation.
Not an Indian dam; learn as upstream China-Brahmaputra strategic issue.

oneLiners: Tehri Dam is on the Bhagirathi River in Uttarakhand.
Bhakra Nangal is linked with the Sutlej River.
Hirakud Dam is on the Mahanadi River in Odisha.
Sardar Sarovar Dam is on the Narmada River in Gujarat.
Indira Sagar Dam is on the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh.
Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is on the Krishna River.
Srisailam Dam is also on the Krishna River.
Mettur Dam is on the Kaveri River in Tamil Nadu.
Krishna Raja Sagar Dam is on the Kaveri River in Karnataka.
Idukki Dam is on the Periyar River in Kerala.
Mullaperiyar Dam is on the Periyar River and is linked with the Kerala-Tamil Nadu dispute.
Koyna Dam is on the Koyna River in Maharashtra.
Ukai Dam is on the Tapi/Tapti River in Gujarat.
Rihand Dam forms Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar in Uttar Pradesh.
Farakka Barrage is on the Ganga River in West Bengal.
Baglihar, Salal, Ratle and Dulhasti are linked with the Chenab River.
Pakal Dul is on the Marusudar, a Chenab tributary.
Kishanganga Project is linked with the Kishanganga/Neelum River.
Subansiri Lower Project is on the Subansiri River in the Northeast.
Polavaram Project is on the Godavari River in Andhra Pradesh.
DVC dams to remember: Maithon, Panchet, Tilaiya and Konar.
Chambal chain: Gandhi Sagar -> Rana Pratap Sagar -> Jawahar Sagar -> Kota Barrage.
Kallanai/Grand Anicut is on the Kaveri River in Tamil Nadu.
Kaveri dispute revision pair: KRS in Karnataka and Mettur in Tamil Nadu.

revisionStrategy.text: Top dams table: learn Dam -> River -> State first.
River groups: Narmada, Krishna, Kaveri, Chenab, Chambal and Damodar.
Superlatives: highest, longest, oldest, arch dam and largest reservoir.
News-linked projects: Subansiri, Ratle, Pakal Dul, Mullaperiyar, Polavaram and Farakka.
Map practice: locate the river and state/UT for each major project.

flashcards.back: River: Sutlej
River: Mahanadi

Tehri Dam is on the Bhagirathi River in Uttarakhand.
Bhakra Nangal is linked with the Sutlej River.
Hirakud Dam is on the Mahanadi River in Odisha.
Sardar Sarovar Dam is on the Narmada River in Gujarat.
Indira Sagar Dam is on the Narmada River in Madhya Pradesh.
Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is on the Krishna River.
Srisailam Dam is also on the Krishna River.
Mettur Dam is on the Kaveri River in Tamil Nadu.
Krishna Raja Sagar Dam is on the Kaveri River in Karnataka.
Idukki Dam is on the Periyar River in Kerala.
Mullaperiyar Dam is on the Periyar River and is linked with the Kerala-Tamil Nadu dispute.
Koyna Dam is on the Koyna River in Maharashtra.
Ukai Dam is on the Tapi/Tapti River in Gujarat.
Rihand Dam forms Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar in Uttar Pradesh.
Farakka Barrage is on the Ganga River in West Bengal.
Baglihar, Salal, Ratle and Dulhasti are linked with the Chenab River.
Pakal Dul is on the Marusudar, a Chenab tributary.
Kishanganga Project is linked with the Kishanganga/Neelum River.
Subansiri Lower Project is on the Subansiri River in the Northeast.
Polavaram Project is on the Godavari River in Andhra Pradesh.
DVC dams to remember: Maithon, Panchet, Tilaiya and Konar.
Chambal chain: Gandhi Sagar -> Rana Pratap Sagar -> Jawahar Sagar -> Kota Barrage.
Kallanai/Grand Anicut is on the Kaveri River in Tamil Nadu.
Kaveri dispute revision pair: KRS in Karnataka and Mettur in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Bhakra Nangal Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Very frequent; multipurpose irrigation and power project.

questions.question: Bhakra Nangal Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Sutlej. Very frequent; multipurpose irrigation and power project.

questions.question: Hirakud Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Longest major earthen dam association; early multipurpose project.

questions.question: Hirakud Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Mahanadi. Longest major earthen dam association; early multipurpose project.

questions.question: Sardar Sarovar Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Narmada Valley Project; linked with Narmada Bachao Andolan.

questions.question: Sardar Sarovar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Narmada. Narmada Valley Project; linked with Narmada Bachao Andolan.

questions.question: Tehri Dam is on which river?
Tehri Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Bhagirathi. Highest/tallest dam in India.

questions.question: Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is on which river?
Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Krishna. Major irrigation and hydropower project.

questions.question: Srisailam Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Important Krishna basin hydropower and irrigation project.

questions.question: Srisailam Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Krishna. Important Krishna basin hydropower and irrigation project.

questions.question: Tungabhadra Dam is on which river?
Tungabhadra Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Tungabhadra. Krishna basin; common exam mapping.

questions.question: Almatti / Lal Bahadur Shastri Dam is on which river?
Almatti / Lal Bahadur Shastri Dam is in which state/UT?
Mettur Dam / Stanley Reservoir is on which river?
Mettur Dam / Stanley Reservoir is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Kaveri. Kaveri water-sharing dispute relevance.

questions.question: Krishna Raja Sagar Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Kaveri basin; often asked with Mettur.

questions.question: Krishna Raja Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Kaveri. Kaveri basin; often asked with Mettur.

questions.question: Kabini Dam is on which river?
Kabini Dam is in which state/UT?
Harangi Dam is on which river?
Harangi Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Harangi. Kaveri basin revision point.

questions.question: Idukki Dam is on which river?
Idukki Dam is in which state/UT?
Mullaperiyar Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Located in Kerala; operated/maintained by Tamil Nadu; dispute in news.

questions.question: Mullaperiyar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Periyar. Located in Kerala; operated/maintained by Tamil Nadu; dispute in news.

questions.question: Koyna Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: Major hydroelectric project in western India.

questions.question: Koyna Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Koyna. Major hydroelectric project in western India.

questions.question: Rihand Dam / Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar is on which river?
Rihand Dam / Govind Ballabh Pant Sagar is in which state/UT?
Gandhi Sagar Dam is on which river?
questions.explanation: First major dam in Chambal chain.

questions.question: Gandhi Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Chambal. First major dam in Chambal chain.

questions.question: Rana Pratap Sagar Dam is on which river?
Rana Pratap Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
Jawahar Sagar Dam is on which river?
Jawahar Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
Kota Barrage is on which river?
questions.explanation: Barrage in the Chambal chain; not a dam.

questions.question: Kota Barrage is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Chambal. Barrage in the Chambal chain; not a dam.

questions.question: Ukai Dam is on which river?
Ukai Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Tapi / Tapti. Important west-flowing river.

questions.question: Maithon Dam is on which river?
Maithon Dam is in which state/UT?
Panchet Dam is on which river?
Panchet Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Damodar. DVC; often paired with Damodar.

questions.question: Tilaiya Dam is on which river?
Tilaiya Dam is in which state/UT?
Konar Dam is on which river?
Konar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Konar. DVC; easy one-name pair.

questions.question: Indira Sagar Dam is on which river?
Indira Sagar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Narmada. Largest reservoir/storage capacity association.

questions.question: Omkareshwar Dam is on which river?
Omkareshwar Dam is in which state/UT?
Bansagar Dam is on which river?
Bansagar Dam is in which state/UT?
Salal Dam is on which river?
Salal Dam is in which state/UT?
Baglihar Dam is on which river?
Baglihar Dam is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Chenab. India-Pakistan water issue association.

questions.question: Dulhasti Dam is on which river?
Dulhasti Dam is in which state/UT?
Ratle Project is on which river?
Ratle Project is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Chenab. Indus Waters Treaty/current-affairs relevance.

questions.question: Pakal Dul Project is on which river?
Pakal Dul Project is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Marusudar / Chenab basin. Major Chenab basin hydropower project.

questions.question: Kishanganga Project is on which river?
Kishanganga Project is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Kishanganga / Neelum. India-Pakistan water dispute association.

questions.question: Subansiri Lower Project is on which river?
Subansiri Lower Project is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Subansiri. Large 2,000 MW hydropower project.

questions.question: Karcham Wangtoo is on which river?
Karcham Wangtoo is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Sutlej. Major private hydropower project.

questions.question: Polavaram Project is on which river?
questions.explanation: National project; displacement and environment relevance.

questions.question: Polavaram Project is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Godavari. National project; displacement and environment relevance.

questions.question: Farakka Barrage is on which river?
Farakka Barrage is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Ganga. Barrage; India-Bangladesh water-sharing and navigation.

questions.question: Kallanai / Grand Anicut is on which river?
questions.explanation: Oldest functional water-diversion structure in India.

questions.question: Kallanai / Grand Anicut is in which state/UT?
questions.explanation: River: Kaveri. Oldest functional water-diversion structure in India.

Function: nerves; deficiency/disease: beriberi
Function: skin/mouth; deficiency/disease: cheilosis, mouth cracks
Function: skin/nerves; deficiency/disease: pellagra
Function: blood/RBC formation; deficiency/disease: anaemia
Function: nerves + RBC; deficiency/disease: pernicious anaemia
Function: gums/wound healing; deficiency/disease: scurvy
Function: bones/calcium absorption; deficiency/disease: rickets/osteomalacia
Function: reproductive health/antioxidant; deficiency/disease: sterility rare
Function: blood clotting; deficiency/disease: excess bleeding
Function: thyroid gland; deficiency/disease: goitre
Function: haemoglobin; deficiency/disease: anaemia
Function: bones/teeth; deficiency/disease: weak bones, osteoporosis
Function: produces bile; deficiency/disease: jaundice, hepatitis
Function: filters blood, forms urine; deficiency/disease: nephritis, kidney stone
Function: pumps blood; deficiency/disease: heart attack, hypertension

Function: control centre; deficiency/disease: stroke, meningitis, epilepsy
Function: digestion/absorption; deficiency/disease: ulcer, diarrhoea, cholera, typhoid

items.fact: India to host 11th BRICS Energy Ministers' Meeting - Gurugram, 25-26 June 2026.
BRICS NSA Meeting under India's chairship - 22-23 June 2026.
International Day of Yoga 2026 - celebrated on 21 June; important national + international event.
PM attended G7 Summit 2026 in France; India was invited as partner country.
India-Slovakia Joint Statement - released during PM visit in June 2026.
India-Africa Forum Summit IV - hosted in New Delhi in 2026.
India-Myanmar Joint Statement 2026 - focused on trade, economy, defence and security.
India's space economy projected around USD 45 billion in next decade.
India has 400+ space startups - important for startup/current affairs.
India's startup ecosystem generated nearly 25 lakh jobs.
India's bioeconomy rose from about $10 billion to $190 billion in 12 years.
Seven CSIR technologies licensed to industry - science-tech current affairs.
Quantum sensing components delivered to DRDO - defence-tech point.
ISRO START 2026 theme: Observation from Space.
ISRO-IIG Space Weather Summer School 2026 held at IIG, Mumbai.
ISRO PSLV-C62 / EOS-N1 Mission launched on 12 Jan 2026.
NISAR mission was launched by GSLV-F16 on 30 July 2025; still important for 2026 exams.
Gaganyaan remains India's key human spaceflight mission.
Indian Microgravity Experiments IMEx-2026 linked to Gaganyaan/human spaceflight research.
SPADEX remains important for docking technology and future space station plans.
Public Sector Banks FY 2025-26 showed strong profitability and improved asset quality.
RBI Financial Stability Report - remember: released by RBI, HQ Mumbai.
GST Council / GST updates - always important for banking GA.
UPI, CBDC, digital payments - RBI-regulated payment ecosystem.
Financial inclusion + Jan Dhan + DBT remain high-yield banking themes.
India's forex reserves, inflation, repo rate - revise monthly before exam.
NABARD - rural/agriculture credit; important for Bank + RRB.
SIDBI - MSME finance; important for banking awareness.
SEBI - capital market regulator; HQ Mumbai.
IRDAI - insurance regulator; HQ Hyderabad.
India won FIH Women's Nations Cup 2026 by defeating New Zealand 2-0.
This win gave India promotion to FIH Pro League.
India completed unbeaten campaign in FIH Women's Nations Cup 2026.
Shubman Gill won first ODI series as India captain against Afghanistan.
India defeated Afghanistan 3-0 in ODI series.
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi scored 94 off 29 balls in India A tri-series final.
India A won tri-series title by defeating Sri Lanka A.
World Yogasana Championship 2026 held in Ahmedabad.
India topped World Yogasana Championship with 114 medals.
Maharaja Trophy KSCA T20 2026 began in Mysuru.
Artemis Programme - NASA Moon mission; revise Artemis II/III.
China's Yarlung Tsangpo mega dam - important for India-Brahmaputra geopolitics.
Red Sea shipping crisis - linked to Bab-el-Mandeb, Suez route, global trade.
South China Sea dispute - China, Philippines, ASEAN; major trade route.
Russia-Ukraine war - energy, food security, NATO relevance.
Israel-Gaza / West Asia tensions - oil, Red Sea, global diplomacy.
BRICS expansion + India chairship events - important international grouping.
G7 2026 - India participated as partner country.
AI, semiconductors, quantum tech - top technology current affairs themes.
Green energy + climate finance + EVs + solar mission - top environment/economy theme for SSC/Bank.
